summary: Broad item decomp pass that replaced many placeholder stubs with matched C implementations, tightened item attribute/item-var structs, and updated headers from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to typed prototypes. The decomp.dev report recorded +8716 matched code bytes, +48 matched data bytes, and 51 new matches, mostly under src/melee/it/items. The work centered on common and character items such as Flipper, Tools, White Bear, Hinoarashi, Arwing Laser, Game & Watch Chef/Parachute, Yoshi Egg Throw, Mewtwo Shadow Ball, Tincle, SS Scope, Patapata, Klap, and Zako Green Shell.
what_changed: Implemented numerous previously stubbed item functions marked with /// #..., including it_80290C38, itFlipper_UnkMotion3_Phys, itFlipper_UnkMotion3_Coll, it_80291254, it_3F14_Logic20_Clanked, and it_3F14_Logic20_HitShield in itflipper.c.

Added Tools item spawn/setup/collision/reaction logic: it_802EEFA8, it_802EF098, it_802EF320, itTools_UnkMotion9_Coll, it_2725_Logic22_Clanked, it_2725_Logic22_HitShield, and it_2725_Logic22_Absorbed.

Added White Bear behavior functions it_802E37BC, it_802E3AC8, itWhitebea_UnkMotion1_Phys, and it_802E3ED0, using facing direction, floor/collision helpers, random timers, and typed WhiteBea attributes.
Added Hinoarashi item vars/attributes and behavior for spawn/init, motion 1 animation, damage received transition, accessory/effect setup, and effect spawning via efSync_Spawn.
Added item spawn helper implementations for Game & Watch Chef it_802C837C, Game & Watch Parachute it_802C6C38, Tools it_802EEFA8, and Yoshi Egg Throw it_802B2A10 using SpawnItem and Item_80268B18.

Added Game & Watch Parachute animation removal/check logic in itGamewatchparachute_UnkMotion1_Anim, including owner callbacks ftGw_AttackAirN_ItemParachuteSetFlag and ftGw_AttackAirN_ItemCheckParachuteRemove.

Added Arwing Laser cleanup/effect/animation helpers, including relationship nulling between linked item gobjs, Vec3 position storage, lb_8000B1CC bone position lookup, efSync_Spawn, and Item_8026AE84 audio/effect call.
Added Mewtwo Shadow Ball destroy and shield-bounce logic, including owner cleanup for Mewtwo/Kirby variants and angle normalization with atan2f and M_TAU after lbVector_Mirror.
Added Patapata, Tincle, SS Scope, Klap, Climbers String, and Zako Green Shell matched functions or prototype/header improvements.

Refined struct layouts in itCharItems.h and itCommonItems.h: split padding into typed fields for itClimbersIceAttributes, Arwing Laser item vars as Vec3 xE18/xE24, itFlipper_DatAttrs fields through x24, itTincle item vars/attributes, itMewtwoShadowball_ItemVars x2C as HSD_GObj*, and itWhiteBeaAttributes s16 fields x8/xA/xC/xE.

Updated headers to replace generic unknown prototypes with typed signatures, e.g. it_802C1854(void) returns bool, it_8028EB88 returns Item_GObj*, it_80290C38 takes Item_GObj*/Vec3*/f32, it_802EEFA8 returns Item_GObj*, and it_802EF098 takes Item_GObj*/s32.
smart_moves: Used struct-layout discoveries to unlock multiple matches: e.g. converting Arwing Laser six s32 slots to two Vec3s made subsequent position-copy code natural and matchable.

Kept item spawn helpers consistent with existing project patterns: fill SpawnItem fields explicitly, zero velocity components, set parent fields and x44_flag.b0, call Item_80268B18, then attach/link with item-specific helpers only if the gobj is non-NULL.
Used small wrapper/outline functions and stack padding where needed for matching, notably it_80291D38_outline plus PAD_STACK in SS Scope and PAD_STACK in several reaction handlers.
Used inline helper extraction in itTools_UnkMotion9_Coll_inline to share the repeated collision landing transition while still preserving matching-sensitive structure.
Preserved duplicated-looking logic in paired callbacks such as Flipper Clanked/HitShield and Tools Clanked/HitShield/Absorbed rather than over-abstracting in ways that could disturb codegen.
Updated headers immediately alongside implementations, reducing future reliance on UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS and making item/fighter/effect cross-calls type-checkable.
decomp_lessons: When an item var block repeatedly copies three adjacent scalars, test whether the underlying layout is a Vec3 rather than separate s32/f32 fields; this PR did that for Arwing Laser xE18/xE24.

Item attributes often start as padding but should be split incrementally as functions reveal exact scalar use; this PR refined Flipper, Tincle, WhiteBea, Climbers Ice, Game & Watch Parachute, and Hinoarashi attributes.
Matched item spawn routines usually require exact SpawnItem field ordering and explicit zeroing of all velocity components, even when semantically redundant.
Owner cleanup code for character items must account for fighter-specific callbacks and variant item kinds, as seen in Climbers String owner cleanup and Mewtwo/Kirby Shadow Ball cleanup.

For collision/reaction item code, preserving the exact ordering of velocity updates, state changes, animation changes, and effect cleanup calls matters; many functions here are short but order-sensitive.
Facing direction updates often require both item facing_dir and mpCollSetFacingDir on x378_itemColl, with -1.0f mapped to -1 and all other cases to 1.

Effect-bearing items commonly pair state transitions with efLib_PauseAll/ResumeAll, efLib_DestroyAll, efSync_Spawn, or efAsync_Spawn; include the correct effect headers rather than leaving placeholders.
naming_conventions: Used existing item motion naming style for typed matches: itFlipper_UnkMotion3_Phys, itFlipper_UnkMotion3_Coll, itGamewatchparachute_UnkMotion1_Anim, itHinoarashi_UnkMotion1_Anim, itPatapata_UnkMotion3_Anim, itWhitebea_UnkMotion1_Phys.
Kept address-derived names where semantic names were not known, such as it_80290C38, it_802EBD14, it_802EEFA8, it_802E37BC, and it_802B2C38.
Kept project item callback naming patterns: LogicXX_DmgDealt, LogicXX_Clanked, LogicXX_HitShield, LogicXX_Absorbed, LogicXX_Destroyed, LogicXX_DmgReceived, and LogicXX_ShieldBounced.
Struct fields remain offset-style names when semantics are unknown, e.g. x0, x4, xC, xDD4_itemVar.*.x60, x64, xE1C_b0, matching the project convention.
Attribute structs use item-specific typedefs with item name prefixes, e.g. itFlipper_DatAttrs, itHinoarashiAttributes, itGamewatchparachuteAttributes, itWhiteBeaAttributes.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Inserted PAD_STACK with specific byte counts in several functions to satisfy stack layout/codegen, including Flipper reaction handlers, Game & Watch spawn helpers, Tools functions, SS Scope, Tincle, WhiteBea, and Yoshi Egg Throw.
Used #pragma dont_inline around Flipper Clanked/HitShield, indicating inlining control was required for matching those callbacks.
Introduced a static outline wrapper it_80291D38_outline for SS Scope so it_80291FA8 calls through a separate function and matches expected codegen.
Used static inline helper itTools_UnkMotion9_Coll_inline to reproduce a repeated transition sequence while matching the collision callback shape.
Preserved explicit float constants and casts such as 1.0f, 0.0f, 0.2f, M_TAU, and casts between s32/f32 for life timers and attributes.
Maintained exact switch/case and if-else structures in item cleanup and collision response code, including null checks before owner/link cleanup.
Used typed prototypes in headers to let compiler calls match expected ABI instead of relying on UNK_PARAMS.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the slice.
The only visible PR comment was the decomp.dev bot report showing GALE01 progress: matched code 58.97% (+0.22%, +8716 bytes), matched data 37.69% (+48 bytes), and 51 new matches.

The bot report highlighted major new item matches including itgamewatchparachute it_802C6C38, itgamewatchchef it_802C837C, ityoshieggthrow it_802B2C38, itwhitebea it_802E3AC8/it_802E3ED0, itflipper functions, ittools functions, ithinoarashi itHinoarashi_UnkMotion1_Anim, ittincle it_802EBD14, and itzgshell functions.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that rename or semantically identify offset-style fields introduced here, especially itFlipper_DatAttrs x8-x24, itHinoarashiAttributes x0/x4/x8, itWhiteBeaAttributes x8/xA/xC/xE, and itTincleAttributes x24/x28/x2C.
Search for follow-up work on remaining stubs in the same files, such as it_80290A7C, it_80290CE8, it_80291DAC, it_802EF548, it_802DDEB4, and Yoshi Egg Throw functions after it_802B2C38.
Compare spawn helper patterns from this PR with future character item spawns to reuse SpawnItem initialization ordering and parent/link calls.
Check whether any later review or cleanup replaced PAD_STACK/#pragma dont_inline tactics with cleaner struct/prototype fixes.
Investigate decomp.dev-reported incidental grcorneria/grhomerun/greatbay matching improvements if they were caused by shared headers or unrelated context not shown in the changed-file list.
